data.title: Bitcoin to stay flat week-on-week amid tariff storm
data.description:
Bitcoin (BTC-USD) is set to stay flat on a weekly basis and remained above the $80K mark as it largely managed to weather the latest tariff-driven volatility and fears of a potential global trade war.

“The crypto markets have managed well in reaction to Trump's historical tariffs, with Bitcoin hovering in range instead of making new lows. This may show a sign of strength, as digital assets are less directly impacted by tariffs due to their decentralized and international accessibility,” Nick Ruck, director at LVRG Research, said.

While the Trump tariffs put pressure on global trade, crypto might come out as a viable safe haven for the future, Ruck added.

The cryptocurrency initially kicked off Monday's session with an extended slump, only to reverse course and trade in positive territory. The week started off with increasing uncertainty driven by speculations about U.S. President Donald Trump’s April 2 tariffs.

BTC inched higher on Tuesday, as the markets geared up for the upcoming tariff announcement. On the tariff day, Bitcoin (BTC-USD) swung between gains and losses on Wednesday after Trump announced that he's enacting new tariffs during his highly anticipated speech. The cryptocurrency rose as high as $88K, and as low as $82K on the day.

Contending that the tariffs will increase competition and bring down prices, Trump announced a minimum baseline levy of 10%. The highest tariff rate is 49%. And he reiterated that the U.S. will impose a 25% levy on all foreign-made automobiles, starting midnight tonight.

U.S. equities on Thursday slumped following the tariff plans, which several analysts dubbed “more aggressive than anticipated.” However, BTC seemed to hold its own and traded between $81K to $83K.

U.S. stocks were mauled Thursday, with the S&P 500 closing in a correction as Trump's reciprocal tariffs stoked fears of a global trade war and potential recession.

“Several scenarios for Bitcoin include it holding up better than equities, rallying during market consolidation, or detaching from the stock market,” Seeking Alpha analyst Nicholas Thomas said.

Risk management is crucial, as Bitcoin could still see significant declines, potentially dropping to the $50k-60k range, Thomas added.

“BTC will start to outperform U.S. risk assets from here on, given the global re-think of U.S. as the preferred capital destination and the Federal Reserve likely being forced into a dovish bias once again,” Augustine Fan, Head of Insights, SignalPlus said.

Finally, as the administration presses on with their stablecoin frameworks and grander crypto policies forward, this will provide positive tailwinds to BTC as adoption and awareness continue to increase, pushing more and more folks to consider Bitcoin as a viable hedge against further de-globalization developments, Fan added.

More Notable News

Riot Platforms (RIOT) mined 533 Bitcoin (BTC-USD) in March, up 13% from the 470 generated in February and 25% from the 425 produced a year ago.

MARA (MARA) produced 829 BTCs in March, up 17% from February 2025.

Cango (CANG) said it will sell its existing business in the PRC to Ursalpha Digital for ~$351.94 million in cash.

CME (CME) is planning to roll out spot-quoted futures contracts on June 30, allowing market participants to trade benchmark crypto and equity index futures in spot-market terms.

BlackRock (BLK) secured registration on the U.K.'s crypto register, enabling the world's largest asset manager to act as an arranger for its iShares Digital Assets AG business, which issues crypto-focused exchange-traded products.

Clear Street recently initiated coverage on Strategy (MSTR) shares with a Buy rating as the Michael Saylor-founded Bitcoin (BTC-USD) development company's "ability to grow its BTC per share, net of dilution, creates real value for shareholders, beyond asset class exposure."

Bitfarms (BITF) entered into an initial agreement for a private debt facility for up to $300M from a Macquarie Group (OTCPK:MCQEF) (OTCPK:MQBKY) division, and expects to use the proceeds for the first part of its Panther Creek data center project, the company said on Wednesday.

Circle Internet, the issuer of USDC (USDC-USD) stablecoin, filed an S-1 registration statement with the SEC for a long-awaited initial public offering.

Tether, the company behind the USDT (USDT-USD) stablecoin, purchased 8,888 Bitcoins (BTC-USD) during the last quarter of 2025, according to recent on-chain transaction data.

Japan's financial regulator is set to propose new legislation that would reclassify crypto assets like Bitcoin (BTC-USD) and Ether (ETH-USD) as financial products.

Crypto moves

Bitcoin (BTC-USD) up 3.1% to $84.3K as of 2:08 pm on Friday. Ether (ETH-USD) was up 2.3% to $1.8K, while DOGE (DOGE-USD) up 8.8%.

data.title: RBC cuts S&P target by 11% to 5,500, driven by rising growth scare amid Trump tariffs
data.description:
RBC Capital Markets sliced its S&P 500 (SP500) target to 5,500 for this year, an 11% chop as the firm sees economic-growth worries heating up in the face of tariffs U.S. President Donald Trump is imposing on U.S. imports worldwide. 

The 5,500 level is a drop from RBC’s previous 6,200 outlook, marking the firm's second benchmark (SP500) downgrade this year. “With this move, our old bear case for the index this year has become our new base case,” Lori Calvasina, head of U.S. equity strategy at RBC Capital Markets, said in a Friday note.

Calvasina outlined four tiers of current “fear” in U.S. equity markets as a way to consider potential downside for the S&P 500 (SP500) as periods of stress and uncertainty emerge. “Unfortunately, immediately following Wednesday’s Rose Garden tariff announcement, the S&P 500 (SP500) quickly and decisively exited tier 1 by ending Thursday more than 12% below its February-2025 peak,” she said. 

With the S&P 500 (SP500) sliding, a “growth scare similar to the 2010, 2011, 2015-2016, and 2018 drawdowns is underway,” Calvasina said. In a Tier 2 scenario, the benchmark (SP500) could experience a 14%-20% drawdown that would push it down to 4,900-5,300. 

The S&P 500 (SP500) on Friday was down ~16% from its all-time closing high of 6,144.15 logged in mid-February. RBC's 5,500 view represents ~7% upside from ~5,150 the benchmark (SP500) hit during Friday afternoon trade. 

RBC now assumes real 2025 GDP growth of 0.1%-1%, slower than its previous 1.1%-1.2% expansion view. 

U.S. stocks underwent growth-scare periods in 2010, with the European debt crisis; in 2011, with the downgrade of U.S. debt; in 2015-2016, with an industrial recession; and in 2018, with a trade war and fears about the Federal Reserve’s balance sheet, RBC said. 

ETFs providing exposure to the S&P 500 include: (NYSEARCA:SPY), (NYSEARCA:VOO), (NYSEARCA:IVV), (RSP), (SSO), (UPRO), (SH), (SDS), and (SPXU).

data.title: Toy story: Mattel may have more tariff risk than Hasbro and Spin Master
data.description:
The rough week for the toy sector continued on Friday, with Mattel (NASDAQ:MAT) -1.2%, Hasbro (NASDAQ:HAS) -2.2%, Spin Master (OTCPK:SNMSF) (TSX:TOY:CA) -1.2% all falling again. Over the last week, Mattel (NASDAQ:MAT) is down 14%, Hasbro (NASDAQ:HAS) has peeled off 11%, and Spin Master is 9.1% lower. 

Clearly, the potential impact of tariffs is front and center in the minds of investors. Toy prices are expected to rise sharply due to the compounded tariffs on imports from China (54%) and Vietnam (46%), which are major manufacturing hubs for toys. "Everyone is really in scramble mode," warned Greg Ahearn, president and CEO of The Toy Association. "This is going to have massive negative repercussions for the consumer and for our industry," he added.

The main concerns are that higher prices could lead to a sharp decline in sales volume and changes to manufacturing bases could cut deeply into profitability.

Weighing in on the developments, Jefferies analyst Kylie Cohu said Mattel (MAT) has the most tariff risk in the sector and Hasbro the least. "MAT has the most exposure to Southeast Asia. Meanwhile, HAS does source some high-margin WotC products from the US," highlighted Cohu. She noted Spin Master (TSX:TOY:CA) appears fairly middle ground in terms of tariff exposure, with its exposure to the European Union.

Jefferies kept Buy ratings on Mattel (MAT), Hasbro (HAS), and Spin Master (OTCPK:SNMSF).

data.title: European pharma evaluates ways to mitigate impact of tariff uncertainty
data.description:
Pharmaceuticals stocks breathed a temporary sigh of relief after U.S. President Donald Trump spared the sector from the ambit of sweeping tariffs imposed during the week.

The pharmaceutical sector has been protected from tariffs, by a Pharma Agreement signed by the World Trade Organization in 1994, for more than three decades. However, this could change as President Trump, during a cabinet meeting in March, said tariffs on pharmaceuticals would begin in the "not too distant" future, with the goal of shifting overseas production of medications back to the U.S.

Big pharma with significant operational footprints in Ireland, Germany, and Switzerland could bear the brunt of the potential tariffs, said global consultancy firm PwC said, in its recent report.

As worries of a potential tariff announcement persists, companies have started exploring strategies to mitigate the impacts.

“Tariffs will likely be problematic for a host of companies importing their drugs into the U.S.,” said Edmund Ingham, Investing Group Leader for Haggerston BioHealth, cautioning that apart from hitting profit margins, tariffs might damage innovation as companies will become more risk-averse and have less money for R&D.

Ingham added that talking of tariff impacts, companies including Jazz Pharmaceuticals (NASDAQ:JAZZ), Perrigo Company (NYSE:PRGO), Alkermes (NASDAQ:ALKS), Novo Nordisk (NVO), AstraZeneca (NASDAQ:AZN), Sanofi (NASDAQ:SNY), GSK (NYSE:GSK), Novartis (NYSE:NVS) and Avadel (NASDAQ:AVDL) should feel the heat.

Switzerland-based Novartis (NYSE:NVS) said it is reviewing the new U.S. tariff policy and assessing its potential impact, while Germany-headquartered pharma giant Merck (NYSE:MRK) said over the past few months, it has analyzed various scenarios and developed “action plans that include both proactive and reactive measures.”

The Trump administration announced a 20% tariff on the European Union imports, as part of the new tariff policy.

“As a global company with headquarters and a strong presence in the EU and operating in many regions, we too might be affected by this development. We will now carefully evaluate the announced measures and their impact on our business,” added Merck.

Roche (OTCQX:RHHBF) also said that it is considering additional U.S. investments to continue to meet patients’ needs. Danish firm Novo Nordisk (NVO) declined to comment.

Analysts have also poured thoughts into how these companies can overcome the brunt.

Ingham said pharma have powerful lobbies and may still hope to bring the Trump administration to the negotiating table and figure out an alternative plan that may ultimately suit both parties better.

The industry lobby group Pharmaceutical Research and Manufacturers of America (PhRMA) welcomed Trump’s resolve to encourage domestic manufacturing, with PhRMA Senior Vice President of Public Affairs, saying that it is looking forward to working with the administration.

Seeking Alpha analyst BioCGT Investor said most of the large U.S.-listed European pharmaceutical companies already have significant manufacturing and R&D operations in the States, allowing them to minimize the impact of tariffs.

“Beyond increasing operations and reducing the API imports to the minimum, pharmaceutical companies could benefit from implementing more AI-powered programs, especially targeting supply chain and inventory optimization, to maximize profit margins,” BioCGT Investor added.

American pharmaceutical majors including Eli Lilly (LLY) have already announced plans to double its recent investments in U.S. domestic medicine production to over $50B, while Johnson & Johnson (JNJ) said it plans to boost its U.S. investments to more than $55B over the next four years.

data.title: BV Financial announces stock repurchase program
data.description:
• BV Financial (NASDAQ:BVFL) announced on Friday a stock repurchase program for up to 10% of the bank holding company's outstanding shares of common stock.
  • The company has received the non-objection of the Federal Reserve Bank of Richmond for the repurchases.
  • The company has ~10.59M shares outstanding.
  • The buybacks are expected to commence after BV Financial releases its results of operations for the period ended March 31, and expire on December 31.
  • The repurchases may be made in open market or private transactions, through block trades, or pursuant to any trading plan that may be adopted in accordance with Rule 10b5-1 of the U.S. SEC.
  • The purchase price will be subject to the management's discretion.

data.title: Trump extending TikTok deal deadline by 75 days
data.description:
President Trump says he's signing an order extending this weekend's deadline for a deal to keep TikTok (BDNCE) operating in the United States for 75 more days.

"My Administration has been working very hard on a Deal to SAVE TIKTOK, and we have made tremendous progress," Trump wrote in a post on his social media network Truth Social. "The Deal requires more work to ensure all necessary approvals are signed, which is why I am signing an Executive Order to keep TikTok up and running for an additional 75 days."

"We hope to continue working in Good Faith with China, who I understand are not very happy about our Reciprocal Tariffs (Necessary for Fair and Balanced Trade between China and the U.S.A.!)," he continued.

But "We do not want TikTok to 'go dark.' We look forward to working with TikTok and China to close the Deal."

TikTok -- owned by China's ByteDance (BDNCE) -- was running quickly into a weekend deadline to transfer control to a U.S. bidder in order to avoid a ban in the United States, where it serves 170M users.

The new deal talks are a redux of a deal that almost went through in 2020, then led by Oracle (ORCL). Oracle is still arguably in a pole position to lead a consortium that can complete a deal for TikTok, though a number of names have put in interest, including Andreessen Horowitz and Blackstone (BX), Amazon.com (AMZN), Microsoft (MSFT), AppLovin (APP), and other coalitions.

data.title: Buffett won't comment on markets, economy until Berkshire Hathaway's annual meeting
data.description:
Berkshire Hathaway (NYSE:BRK.B) (NYSE:BRK.A) said all reports on social media regarding comments "allegedly made by Warren E. Buffett... are false."

That was made in response to a video President Donald Trump reposted on his Truth Social feed that claims Buffett described Trump's economic moves as the best he has seen in the last 50 years.

CNBC's Becky Quick said Buffett hasn't commented on and won't comment on the markets, the economy, or tariffs between now and the company's annual meeting on May 3.

Berkshire Hathaway's (NYSE:BRK.A) statement echoed a similar warning it issued in October, days before the presidential election.

At Thursday's close, Berkshire Hathaway's (NYSE:BRK.B) B shares rose 17% year-to-date, outperforming the S&P 500's 12% decline. In early afternoon trading on Friday, BRK.B shares fell 6.2% vs. the S&P's 5.1% drop.

data.title: Klarna, Chime delay planned IPO amid Trump tariff announcement - WSJ (update)
data.description:
Update 1:10pm: Adds Chime details.

Swedish fintech firm Klarna (KLAR) has paused its planned initial public offering amid the stock market tumult with Trump's tariff announcement. 

Klarna (KLAR) was expected to start marketing shares for the IPO on Monday, though decided to postpone the effort this week, according to a WSJ report on Friday, which cited people familiar with the matter.

Klarna (KLAR), "known for buy, now, pay later"  financing, filed a registration statement for its IPO last month. Bloomberg reported last month that Klarna was seeking to raise at least $1B in the IPO and is targeting a valuation of more than $15B in the listing.

Fintech firm Chime is delaying filing its financials publicly and also delaying its IPO, according to the WSJ report.

Ticketing marketplace StubHub (STUB) is also delaying its planned IPO amid the market jitters, the WSJ reported earlier Friday. The company has put its IPO roadshow on hold, which was scheduled for next week, after the S&P 500 on Thursday saw its worst trading session since the COVID-19 pandemic.

data.title: Banks, fintechs will likely compete and cooperate in gen-AI innovation, Fed's Barr says
data.description:
Fintechs have a role to pay in accelerating responsible innovation at banks in the area of generative artificial intelligence, Federal Reserve Governor Michael S. Barr said on Friday in prepared text of a speech he delivered at the San Francisco Fed.

"Competition and cooperation between banks and fintechs will likely spur innovation and accelerate the integration of Gen AI into banking," he said.

Fintechs are well positioned to integrate generative AI into products and services, and banks have valuable data on which gen-AI models can by optimized, he explained. However, it's imperative to manage the risks involved in implementing a new technology.

"To harness the upsides of Gen AI, banks, fintechs, and regulators all have a role to play in helping to ensure that the risks are managed," Barr said.

Some reasons why gen-AI hasn't been broadly applied in banks are related to the development of the technology, banks' organizational structures, and regulatory requirements, he said.

One reason why fintechs may help to drive the widespread adoption of gen-AI is that they're usually relatively young companies with clean tech stacks, meaning they're not trying to infuse new technology into an old infrastructure.

"Fintechs have the ability to operate at speed but start with no scale," Barr said. "Banks, on the other hand, move more slowly but have scale in terms of investment, consumer reach, and risk management."

In some cases, fintechs and banks will compete with each other in offering gen AI-driven products. In others, they may form collaborative partnerships, merging their strengths. Both banks and fintechs have roles to play in managing the risks.

Regulators will need to stay educated and informed on the technology, Barr said. "They should review and update existing standards on model risk management, as appropriate, and engage in public–private forums where there are opportunities to work together."  In addition, regulators should explore how and when to use the technology themselves.

Fintech related ETFs: Global X FinTech ETF (NASDAQ:FINX), ARK Fintech Innovation ETF (BATS:ARKF), iShares FinTech Active ETF (NYSEARCA:BPAY).

data.title: Wayfair bounces off its 52-week low after positive development with Vietnam tariff scenario
data.description:
Wayfair (NYSE:W) moved off its 52-week low from earlier in the session after President Trump said he had a productive call with Vietnam General Secretary of the Communist Party To Lam in regard to working out an agreement on tariffs.

Notably, the online retailer has higher exposure to Vietnam than some of its competitors. Wayfair (NYSE:W) said on Thursday that it is closely monitoring the evolving trade landscape, and sees itself as well-positioned to continue to offer customers the best possible combination of value, assortment, and experience.

Shares of Wayfair (W) were up 2.2% at 12:50 p.m. to cut into the weekly drop of more than 25% on above-average volume. At last check, Wayfair (W) shares were swapping hands at $25.64 vs. the 52-week range of $20.42 to $76.18. Short interest on Wayfair (W) stands at 17.0% of the total float, which could add to the volatility.
